when someone is spoken to directly, is an official or legally recognized form of reference for a person or other entity (such as a government or company), and may often be used in conjunction with a personal [title](/wiki/Title "Title").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) A style, by [tradition](/wiki/Tradition "Tradition") or [law](/wiki/Law "Law"), precedes a reference to a person who holds a post or [political office](/wiki/Political_office "Political office") and is sometimes used to refer to the office itself. An [honorific](/wiki/Honorific "Honorific") can also be awarded to an individual in a personal capacity. Such styles are particularly associated with [monarchies](/wiki/Monarchy "Monarchy"), where they may be used by a wife of an office holder or of a prince of the blood, for the duration of their [marriage](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage"). They are also almost universally used for [presidents](/wiki/President_(government_title) "President (government title)") in [republics](/wiki/Republic "Republic") and in many countries for [members](/wiki/Legislator "Legislator") of [legislative bodies](/wiki/Legislature "Legislature"), higher-ranking [judges](/wiki/Judge "Judge"), and senior constitutional office holders. Leading [religious](/wiki/Religion "Religion") [figures](/wiki/Leadership "Leadership") also have styles.

Official or legally recognized title for a person or entity

Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Style" form of address](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Style%22+form+of+address) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Style%22+form+of+address+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Style%22+form+of+address&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Style%22+form+of+address+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Style%22+form+of+address) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Style%22+form+of+address&acc=on&wc=on) *(June 2008)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

A **style of office**, also called **manner of reference**, or **form of address** when someone is spoken to directly, is an official or legally recognized form of reference for a person or other entity (such as a government or company), and may often be used in conjunction with a personal [title](/wiki/Title "Title").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) A style, by [tradition](/wiki/Tradition "Tradition") or [law](/wiki/Law "Law"), precedes a reference to a person who holds a post or [political office](/wiki/Political_office "Political office") and is sometimes used to refer to the office itself. An [honorific](/wiki/Honorific "Honorific") can also be awarded to an individual in a personal capacity. Such styles are particularly associated with [monarchies](/wiki/Monarchy "Monarchy"), where they may be used by a wife of an office holder or of a prince of the blood, for the duration of their [marriage](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage"). They are also almost universally used for [presidents](/wiki/President_(government_title) "President (government title)") in [republics](/wiki/Republic "Republic") and in many countries for [members](/wiki/Legislator "Legislator") of [legislative bodies](/wiki/Legislature "Legislature"), higher-ranking [judges](/wiki/Judge "Judge"), and senior constitutional office holders. Leading [religious](/wiki/Religion "Religion") [figures](/wiki/Leadership "Leadership") also have styles.

The second-person (singular and plural) possessive adjective *your* is used as a form of address (that is, when speaking directly to the person[s] entitled to the style[s]); the third-person possessive adjectives *his/her* (singular) and *their* (plural) are used as forms of reference (that is, when speaking about the person[s] entitled to the style[s]).

## Examples

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Examples")]

### Academia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Academia")]

Traditional forms of address at [German](/wiki/German_language "German language")-speaking universities:

* His/Her Magnificence – rector (president) of a university
* His/Her Notability (*Seine Spektabilität*; Professors have the privilege to use the Latin *Spectabilis*) – dean of a faculty

Traditional forms of address at [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language")-speaking universities:

* His/Her Great Honour (*Edelgrootachtbare heer/vrouwe*) – rector magnificus (president) of a university
* Highly Learned Sir/Madam (*Hooggeleerde heer/vrouwe*) – professor or dean of a faculty
* Well (Noble) Very Learned Sir/Madam (*Weledelzeergeleerde heer/vrouwe*) – a doctor
* Well (Noble) Learned Sir/Madam (*De weledelgeleerde heer/vrouwe*) – a [doctorandus](/wiki/Doctorandus "Doctorandus")
* Well (Noble) Strictly Sir/Madam (*De weledelgestrenge heer/vrouwe*) – a master in laws (*meester in de rechten*) or a university engineer (*ingenieur*)

Traditional forms of address at [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language")-speaking universities:

* Magnificent Rector (*magnifico rettore*) – rector (president, chancellor) of a university
* Amplified Headmaster (*amplissimo preside*) – dean of a faculty (now uncommon)
* Illustrious/Enlightened Professor (*chiarissimo professore*) – a full professor

### Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Government")]

#### Diplomats

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Diplomats")]

* His [Most Reverend](/wiki/Most_Reverend "Most Reverend") Excellency (abbreviation *His Most Rev. Ex.*, oral address *Your Excellency*) – [apostolic nuncios](/wiki/Nuncio "Nuncio"), because their rank is equal to that of an [ambassador](/wiki/Ambassador "Ambassador") extraordinary and plenipotentiary, and they are simultaneously higher [prelates](/wiki/Prelate "Prelate").
* His/Her [Excellency](/wiki/Excellency "Excellency") (abbreviation *HE*, oral address *Your Excellency*) – most [ambassadors](/wiki/Ambassador "Ambassador"), [high commissioners](/wiki/High_commissioner "High commissioner") and [permanent representatives](/wiki/Permanent_representative "Permanent representative") to international organizations. Occasionally a different form of address is used domestically with the international equivalent of *Excellency* being used in all other cases.

#### Judiciaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Judiciaries")]

* [His/Her Honour](/wiki/His_Honour "His Honour") Judge X
  + Abbreviation *HHJ X*, oral address *Your Honour* – Circuit judges in [England and Wales](/wiki/England_and_Wales "England and Wales").
  + [The Honourable](/wiki/The_Honourable "The Honourable") Mr./Ms. Justice X (abbreviation *X J*, referential *His Lordship/Her Ladyship*; oral address *My Lord/Lady* or *Your Lordship/Your Ladyship*) – Judges of the [High Court of Justice](/wiki/High_Court_of_Justice "High Court of Justice") of England and Wales.
  + My Lord/Your Lordship is used to address judiciary representatives in [India](/wiki/India "India").
  + The Honorable (abbreviation *The Hon.*, oral address *Your Honor*) – Judges and justices in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
  + Oral address *Your Honour* – All courts in [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia").[[4]](#cite_note-4)
  + [The Right Honourable](/wiki/The_Right_Honourable "The Right Honourable") Lord/Lady Justice X (abbreviation *X LJ*) – Judges of the [Court of Appeal of England and Wales](/wiki/Court_of_Appeal_of_England_and_Wales "Court of Appeal of England and Wales").
* Oral address *Your Excellency* – Judges of the [International Court of Justice](/wiki/International_Court_of_Justice "International Court of Justice").
* Oral address *Your Worship* – All courts in [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") (obsolete).
* The Lord/Lady/Baroness[[5]](#cite_note-5) X (abbreviated to *Lord/Lady/Baroness X*, referred to as *His Lordship/Her Ladyship*, addressed orally as *My Lord/My Lady*) – Judges in the [High Court of Justiciary](/wiki/High_Court_of_Justiciary "High Court of Justiciary") and the [Court of Session](/wiki/Court_of_Session "Court of Session") in Scotland, and the [Supreme Court of the United Kingdom](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_the_United_Kingdom "Supreme Court of the United Kingdom").
* Oral address *[Your Worship](/wiki/Worship_(style) "Worship (style)")* – [Justices of the peace](/wiki/Justice_of_the_peace "Justice of the peace") (magistrates) in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), usually by solicitors.

* [Sire](/wiki/Sire "Sire") (oral address first *[Your Majesty](/wiki/Majesty "Majesty")* and then *Sire*; for a queen first *Your Majesty* and then *[Ma'am](/wiki/Madam "Madam")*) – Reigning [kings](/wiki/King "King") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") and in [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). It has also been used in [France](/wiki/France "France"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") and [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain").
* His/Her [Imperial Majesty](/wiki/Imperial_Majesty "Imperial Majesty") (abbreviation *HIM*, oral address *Your Imperial Majesty*) – Emperors and empresses. Formerly, for example, HIM the Shah of Iran. In modern times, the [Emperor of Japan](/wiki/Emperor_of_Japan "Emperor of Japan") more often uses the simpler style of "Majesty".[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* His/Her [Imperial and Royal Majesty](/wiki/Imperial_and_Royal_Majesty "Imperial and Royal Majesty") (abbreviation *HI&RM*, oral address *Your Imperial and Royal Majesty*) – Until 1918, the rulers of the [Austro-Hungarian Empire](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary"), who were Emperors/Empresses of Austria while also Kings/Queens of Hungary and Bohemia, and the German emperors/empresses, who were simultaneously [Kings/Queens of Prussia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Prussia "Kingdom of Prussia").
* His/Her [Apostolic Majesty](/wiki/Apostolic_Majesty "Apostolic Majesty") (abbreviation *HAM*, oral address *Your Apostolic Majesty*) – the [King of Hungary](/wiki/King_of_Hungary "King of Hungary"), usually styled Imperial Majesty or Imperial and Royal Majesty as Emperor of Austria and King of Hungary, also sometimes Imperial and Royal Apostolic Majesty.
* His/Her [Britannic Majesty](/wiki/Britannic_Majesty "Britannic Majesty") – the [British monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_the_United_Kingdom "Monarchy of the United Kingdom") (not usual); used as a formal and official term in [diplomacy](/wiki/Diplomacy "Diplomacy"), [international law](/wiki/International_law "International law"), and [international relations](/wiki/International_relations "International relations"), e.g. in [British passports](/wiki/British_passport "British passport").
* His/Her [Most Gracious Majesty](/wiki/Most_Gracious_Majesty "Most Gracious Majesty") – an elaborate version of His/Her Majesty in the United Kingdom, only used in the most formal of occasions.
* His/Her [Most Excellent Majesty](/wiki/Most_Excellent_Majesty "Most Excellent Majesty") – another elaborate version of His/Her Majesty in the United Kingdom, mainly used in [Acts of Parliament](/wiki/Acts_of_Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom_relating_to_the_European_Communities_and_the_European_Union "Acts of Parliament of the United Kingdom relating to the European Communities and the European Union").
* His/Her [Catholic Majesty](/wiki/Rex_Catholicissimus "Rex Catholicissimus") (abbreviation *HCM*, oral address *Your Catholic Majesty*) – the [Spanish monarch](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Spain "Monarchy of Spain") (not usual).
* His [Most Christian Majesty](/wiki/Style_of_the_French_sovereign "Style of the French sovereign") – the [King of France](/wiki/King_of_France "King of France") until 1790 and from 1815 to 1830.
* His/Her [Most Faithful Majesty](/wiki/Most_Faithful_Majesty "Most Faithful Majesty") (abbreviation *HFM*, oral address *Your Faithful Majesty*) – the [King of Portugal](/wiki/List_of_Portuguese_monarchs "List of Portuguese monarchs") until deposed in 1910.
* His/Her [Majesty](/wiki/Majesty "Majesty") (abbreviation *HM*, oral address *Your Majesty*) – kings, queens and some sultans. For example, HM King [Charles III](/wiki/Charles_III "Charles III"), HM King [Mohammed VI](/wiki/Mohammed_VI_of_Morocco "Mohammed VI of Morocco") or HM King [Willem-Alexander](/wiki/Willem-Alexander_of_the_Netherlands "Willem-Alexander of the Netherlands").
* His/Her [Imperial Highness](/wiki/Imperial_Highness "Imperial Highness") (abbreviation *HIH*, oral address *Your Imperial Highness*) – members of an imperial house. Currently used by the [Imperial House of Japan](/wiki/Imperial_House_of_Japan "Imperial House of Japan").
* His/Her [Imperial and Royal Highness](/wiki/Imperial_and_Royal_Highness "Imperial and Royal Highness") (abbreviation *HI&RH*, oral address *Your Imperial and Royal Highness*) – formerly, [archdukes](/wiki/Archduke "Archduke") of the [House of Habsburg](/wiki/House_of_Habsburg "House of Habsburg"), the German crown prince/princess and (post-monarchy) members of the deposed [Brazilian Imperial Family](/wiki/Brazilian_imperial_family "Brazilian imperial family"); also some women entitled to imperial style by birth and to royal style by marriage (e.g., [Grand Duchess Maria Alexandrovna of Russia](/wiki/Grand_Duchess_Maria_Alexandrovna_of_Russia "Grand Duchess Maria Alexandrovna of Russia"), Duchess of Edinburgh).
* His/Her [Royal Highness](/wiki/Royal_Highness "Royal Highness") (abbreviation *HRH*, oral address *Your Royal Highness*) – some monarchs, members of a [royal family](/wiki/Royal_family "Royal family") (other than monarchs, [queens consort](/wiki/Queen_consort "Queen consort") and [queens dowager](/wiki/Queen_dowager "Queen dowager")); [grand dukes](/wiki/Grand_duke "Grand duke")/duchesses who have [reigned](/wiki/Reign "Reign") (but not those grand dukes who were [cadets](/wiki/Cadet_(genealogy) "Cadet (genealogy)") of the former Russian Imperial Family), consorts of grand dukes, grand ducal [heirs apparent](/wiki/Heir_apparent "Heir apparent") and, in Luxembourg, all dynastic male-line cadets; [British princes](/wiki/British_prince "British prince"), their [dynastic](/wiki/Dynasty "Dynasty") wives (including the prince consort of queens [Victoria](/wiki/Queen_Victoria "Queen Victoria") and [Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II")), sons, daughters, [patrilineal](/wiki/Patrilineality "Patrilineality") grandsons and granddaughters of [Ibn Saud](/wiki/Ibn_Saud "Ibn Saud") of the [House of Saud](/wiki/House_of_Saud "House of Saud").
* [His/Her Grace](/wiki/Grace_(style) "Grace (style)") (oral address *Your Grace*) is a style used for various high-ranking personages. It was the style used to address [Kings of England](/wiki/List_of_English_monarchs "List of English monarchs") until King [Henry VIII](/wiki/Henry_VIII "Henry VIII")[[8]](#cite_note-Pollard2007-8) and the [King or Queen of Scots](/wiki/List_of_Scottish_monarchs "List of Scottish monarchs") up to the [Act of Union of 1707](/wiki/Acts_of_Union_1707 "Acts of Union 1707"), which united the [Kingdom of Scotland](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Scotland "Kingdom of Scotland") and the [Kingdom of England](/wiki/Kingdom_of_England "Kingdom of England"). Today, the style is used when referring to non-royal dukes and duchesses, and archbishops, in the United Kingdom. For example, His Grace The [Duke of Devonshire](/wiki/Duke_of_Devonshire "Duke of Devonshire") in the United Kingdom, or His Grace The [Archbishop of Canterbury](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Canterbury "Archbishop of Canterbury"); or *Your Grace* in spoken or written address. [Royal dukes](/wiki/Duke#Royal_dukes "Duke"), for example the [Duke of Edinburgh](/wiki/Prince_Edward,_Duke_of_Edinburgh "Prince Edward, Duke of Edinburgh"), are addressed with their higher royal style, *[Royal Highness](/wiki/Royal_Highness "Royal Highness")*.
* His/Her [Grand Ducal Highness](/wiki/Grand_Ducal_Highness "Grand Ducal Highness") (abbreviation *HGDH*, oral address *Your Grand Ducal Highness*) – cadets of some former ruling grand ducal dynasties ([Hesse and by Rhine](/wiki/Grand_Duchy_of_Hesse "Grand Duchy of Hesse") and [Baden](/wiki/Grand_Duchy_of_Baden "Grand Duchy of Baden")).
* His/Her [Highness](/wiki/Highness "Highness") (abbreviation *HH*, oral address, *Your Highness*) – some monarchs, i.e., [emirs](/wiki/Emir "Emir"), some [sultans](/wiki/Sultan "Sultan"), the [Aga Khan](/wiki/Aga_Khan "Aga Khan"); formerly reigning dukes, some [maharajas](/wiki/Maharaja "Maharaja") and [rajas](/wiki/Raja "Raja") and the members of their dynasties; cadets of most former grand ducal houses; male-line grandchildren and remoter male-line descendants of some kingly dynasties (i.e., Denmark and formerly Brazil, Italy, Japan, UK, Yugoslavia); Belgium's [House of Ligne](/wiki/House_of_Ligne "House of Ligne"); members of France's former [Foreign Princely](/wiki/Prince_%C3%A9tranger "Prince étranger") class, members of [cadet branches](/wiki/Cadet_branch "Cadet branch") of the [House of Saud](/wiki/House_of_Saud "House of Saud").
* His/Her [Ducal Serene Highness](/wiki/Ducal_Serene_Highness "Ducal Serene Highness") (abbreviation *HDSH*, oral address, *Your Ducal Serene Highness*) – members of the formerly reigning ducal [House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha](/wiki/House_of_Saxe-Coburg_and_Gotha "House of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha").
* His/Her [Serene Highness](/wiki/Serene_Highness "Serene Highness") (abbreviation *HSH*, oral address *Your Serene Highness*) – German: *Seine/Ihre Durchlaucht*; Italian: *Sua Altezza Serenissima*; Russian: Ваша светлость. Sovereigns of a [principality](/wiki/Principality "Principality") (i.e., Liechtenstein, Monaco); members of formerly reigning princely families (Lippe, Schaumburg-Lippe, Waldeck and Pyrmont and Schwarzburg); members of [mediatized](/wiki/German_mediatisation "German mediatisation") families headed by a *[Fürst](/wiki/F%C3%BCrst "Fürst")* ("prince"); members of several formerly noble, princely families of Austria, Germany, Bohemia, Hungary and Poland; [morganatic](/wiki/Morganatic_marriage "Morganatic marriage") princes, descended from reigning dynasties; and a few formerly noble families granted the princely title in Imperial Russia (the style is more literally translated "His/Her Serenity").
* Her/His [Most Serene Highness](/wiki/Serene_Highness "Serene Highness") (abbreviation *HMSH*, oral address, *Your Most Serene Highness*) – [Prince-electors](/wiki/Prince-elector "Prince-elector") of the [Holy Roman Empire](/wiki/Holy_Roman_Empire "Holy Roman Empire").
* His/Her [Illustrious Highness](/wiki/Illustrious_Highness "Illustrious Highness") (abbreviation *HIllH*, oral address *Your Illustrious Highness*, German: *Seine/Ihre Erlaucht*); Italian: *Sua Altezza Illustrissima*; Spanish: *Su Ilustrísima*; Russian: Ваше сиятельство. Mediatized [counts](/wiki/Count "Count") and, sometimes, members of their families.
* His/Her Princely Grace (*Fürstliche Gnaden*) - former style for members of a few noble families of monarchical Germany.
* The High-born (*[Hochgeboren](/wiki/Hochgeboren "Hochgeboren")*) – counts in some Scandinavian and Benelux monarchies and, formerly, Germany and Austria.
* The High Well-born (*[Hochwohlgeboren](/wiki/Hochwohlgeboren "Hochwohlgeboren")*) – Dutch barons; knights and untitled members of the lower nobility in German-speaking monarchies.
* His/Her [Excellency](/wiki/Excellency "Excellency") (abbreviation *HE*, oral address *Your Excellency*) – [governors-general](/wiki/Governors-general "Governors-general"), British colonial governors, state officials, and generals of Imperial Russia. Occasionally, [prime ministers](/wiki/Prime_minister "Prime minister") and cabinet ministers. For example, the [Prime Minister of the Netherlands](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_the_Netherlands "Prime Minister of the Netherlands"); in Denmark, a few high-ranked nobles (e.g., [Counts af Danneskiold-Samsø](/wiki/Danneskiold-Sams%C3%B8e "Danneskiold-Samsøe"), [Counts of Rosenborg](/wiki/Count_of_Rosenborg "Count of Rosenborg"), *[Lensgrever](/wiki/Lensgreve_(Danish_title) "Lensgreve (Danish title)")*, knights of the [Order of the Elephant](/wiki/Order_of_the_Elephant#Use "Order of the Elephant")).
* *Don* (Spanish: [[don]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish"), Italian: [[dɔn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian"), Portuguese: *Dom* [[dõ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")) from [Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *[dominus](/wiki/Dominus_(title) "Dominus (title)")*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'Lord', is an [honorific](/wiki/Honorific "Honorific") title used in Spain, Portugal, Italy, [Iberoamerica](/wiki/Iberoamerica "Iberoamerica") and the Philippines. The female equivalent is *doña* (Spanish: [[ˈdoɲa]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")), *donna* (Italian: [[ˈdɔnna]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian")), and *dona* (Portuguese: [[ˈdonɐ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")), abbreviated "Dª" or simply "D." In formal settings, it is a title reserved for royalty, select nobles, knights, dames, and church hierarchs. Informally, it is sometimes used as a mark of esteem for a person of personal, social or official distinction, such as a community leader of long standing, or a person of significant wealth. Like the British *Sir*, It is used with, and not instead of, a person's name.
* In Portugal and Brazil, *[Dom](/wiki/Don_(honorific)#Portuguese-speaking_lands "Don (honorific)")* (pronounced [[ˈdõ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")) is used for certain [hierarchs](/wiki/Ordinary_(Catholic_Church) "Ordinary (Catholic Church)") of the Catholic Church and for [laymen](/wiki/Laity "Laity") who belong to the royal and imperial families (for example, the [House of Aviz](/wiki/House_of_Aviz "House of Aviz") in Portugal and the [House of Braganza](/wiki/House_of_Braganza "House of Braganza") in Portugal and Brazil).[[9]](#cite_note-9) It was also accorded to members of families of the titled [Portuguese nobility](/wiki/Portuguese_nobility "Portuguese nobility").[[10]](#cite_note-cedre-10) Unless ennobling [letters patent](/wiki/Letters_patent "Letters patent") specifically authorised its use, *Dom* was not attributed to members of Portugal's untitled nobility. Since hereditary titles in Portugal descended according to [primogeniture](/wiki/Primogeniture "Primogeniture"), the right to the style of *Dom* was the only apparent distinction between [cadets](/wiki/Cadet_(genealogy) "Cadet (genealogy)") of titled families and members of untitled noble families.[[10]](#cite_note-cedre-10)
* Most High, Mighty, and Illustrious Prince – for royal dukes, oral address Your Royal Highness.
* [Son of Heaven](/wiki/Son_of_Heaven "Son of Heaven") – Used by Chinese and some Japanese emperors.
* [High King](/wiki/High_King "High King")
* [Great King](/wiki/Great_King "Great King")
* [King of Kings](/wiki/King_of_Kings "King of Kings") – Used by especially Semitic, Persian and some Indian rulers.
* "Taewang" "Greatest of Kings" – was used by the later rulers of the Korean kingdom of [Koguryo](/wiki/Koguryo "Koguryo").
* [Lord of the Isles](/wiki/Lord_of_the_Isles "Lord of the Isles") – Used by an [heir apparent](/wiki/Heir_apparent "Heir apparent") of the [British monarchy](/wiki/British_monarchy "British monarchy").
* [High Steward of Scotland](/wiki/High_Steward_of_Scotland "High Steward of Scotland") – Used by an [heir apparent](/wiki/Heir_apparent "Heir apparent") of the [British monarchy](/wiki/British_monarchy "British monarchy").
* [Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques](/wiki/Custodian_of_the_Two_Holy_Mosques "Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques") (abbreviation CTHM) ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): خادم الحرمين الشريفين *Khādim al-Ḥaramayn aš-Šarīfayn*), sometimes translated as Servant of the Two Noble Sanctuaries or Protector of the Two Holy Cities, is a [royal style](/wiki/Royal_and_noble_styles "Royal and noble styles") that has been used by many Islamic rulers including the [Ayyubids](/wiki/Ayyubids "Ayyubids"), the [Mamluk Sultans of Egypt](/wiki/Mamluk_Sultans_of_Egypt "Mamluk Sultans of Egypt"), the [Ottoman Sultans](/wiki/Ottoman_Sultans "Ottoman Sultans"), and the modern [Saudi kings](/wiki/King_of_Saudi_Arabia "King of Saudi Arabia").[[11]](#cite_note-bbc-11) The title refers to the ruler taking the responsibility of guarding and maintaining the two holiest [mosques](/wiki/Mosques "Mosques") in [Islam](/wiki/Islam "Islam"), [Al-Masjid al-Haram](/wiki/Al-Masjid_al-Haram "Al-Masjid al-Haram") (the Sacred Mosque) in [Mecca](/wiki/Mecca "Mecca") and [Al-Masjid an-Nabawi](/wiki/Al-Masjid_an-Nabawi "Al-Masjid an-Nabawi") (the Prophet's Mosque) in [Medina](/wiki/Medina "Medina").[[11]](#cite_note-bbc-11)[[12]](#cite_note-emjap-12) In [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia"), it is used as the official title of the king, in place of "His Majesty".
* [Amīr al-Mu'minīn](/wiki/Am%C4%ABr_al-Mu%27min%C4%ABn "Amīr al-Mu'minīn") ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): أمير المؤمنين), usually translated Commander of the Faithful or Leader of the Faithful, is the Arabic style of some [Caliphs](/wiki/Caliphate "Caliphate") and other independent sovereign [Muslim](/wiki/Muslim "Muslim") rulers that claim legitimacy from a community of Muslims. It has been claimed as the title of rulers in Muslim countries and empires and is still used for some Muslim leaders. The use of the title does not necessarily signify a claim to caliphate as it is usually taken to be, but described a certain form of activist leadership which may have been attached to a caliph but also could signify a level of authority beneath that. The Ottoman sultans, in particular, made scant use of it. Moreover, the term was used by men who made no claim to be caliphs.[[13]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-13) Used by the former leader of [ISIS](/wiki/ISIS "ISIS") [Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi](/wiki/Abu_Bakr_al-Baghdadi "Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi"), the Ahmadiyya Muslim leader [Mirza Masroor Ahmad](/wiki/Mirza_Masroor_Ahmad "Mirza Masroor Ahmad"),[[14]](#cite_note-Valentine_208-14) the [King of Morocco](/wiki/King_of_Morocco "King of Morocco"), the [Sultan of Sokoto](/wiki/Sultan_of_Sokoto "Sultan of Sokoto"), and the supreme leaders of the Afghan [Taliban](/wiki/Taliban "Taliban").
* Kabiyesi (variously translated as His or Her Royal Majesty, His or Her Royal Highness or His or Her Highness, lit. *The One whose words are beyond question*) – Used by the [Obas](/wiki/Oba_(ruler) "Oba (ruler)") of [Yorubaland](/wiki/Yorubaland "Yorubaland"), other aboriginal [Yoruba](/wiki/Yoruba_people "Yoruba people") high chiefs of royal background, and their counterparts in the tribe's diaspora communities.

##### Styles and titles of deposed monarchs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Styles and titles of deposed monarchs")]

General tradition indicates that monarchs who have ceased to reign but not renounced their hereditary titles, retain the use of their style and title for the duration of their lifetimes, but both die with them. Hence, prior to his death, Greece's deposed king was still styled *His Majesty [King Constantine II](/wiki/Constantine_II_of_Greece "Constantine II of Greece")*, as a *personal* title, not as occupant of a constitutional office, since the abolition of the monarchy by the Hellenic Republic in 1974. Similarly, until his death, the last King of Italy, [Umberto II](/wiki/Umberto_II_of_Italy "Umberto II of Italy"), was widely referred to as *King Umberto II* and sometimes addressed as *Your Majesty*. In contrast, [Simeon of Bulgaria](/wiki/Simeon_Saxe-Coburg-Gotha "Simeon Saxe-Coburg-Gotha") who, subsequent to the loss of his throne in 1947, was elected to and held the premiership of his former realm as "Simeon Sakskoburggotski", and therefore is as often referred to by the latter name as by his former royal title and style.

While this rule is generally observed, and indeed some exiled monarchs are allowed diplomatic passports by their former realm, other republics officially object to the use of such titles which are, nonetheless, generally accorded by extant monarchical regimes. In 1981, the then Greek President [Konstantinos Karamanlis](/wiki/Konstantinos_Karamanlis "Konstantinos Karamanlis") declined to attend the wedding of [Charles, Prince of Wales](/wiki/Charles,_Prince_of_Wales "Charles, Prince of Wales") when it was revealed that Greece's deposed monarch, a cousin of the Prince, had been referred to as "King" in his invitation. The current Hellenic Republic challenged King Constantine's right to use his title, and his passport was revoked in 1994, because he did not use a surname, as his passport at the time stated "Constantine, former King of the Hellenes". However, Constantine II later travelled in and out of Greece on a [Danish diplomatic passport](/wiki/Danish_passport "Danish passport") as a descendant of [Christian IX of Denmark](/wiki/Christian_IX_of_Denmark "Christian IX of Denmark"), by the name *Constantino de Grecia* ([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language") for "Constantine of Greece").

#### Republics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Republics")]

* His/Her [Excellency](/wiki/Excellency "Excellency") (abbreviation *HE*, oral address *Your Excellency*) – [Presidents](/wiki/President_(government_title) "President (government title)") of republics (historically, this was first used to refer to George Washington during his tenure as Commander-in-Chief of the Army during the American War of Independence; its use for presidents of republics was established as he was the first president of the first modern republic). In some countries also the prime minister, ministers, governors, ambassadors and [high commissioners](/wiki/High_commissioner "High commissioner") also use this style.
* The [president of the United States](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") is properly directly addressed as "Mr./Mrs./Ms./Miss./Sir/Madam President" and introduced as "The President of the United States"; however, His/Her/Your Excellency may properly be used in written communications and is sometimes used in official documents.
* The custom in [France](/wiki/France "France") is to call office holders acting within their official capacity *M. (Monsieur)* or *[Mme](/wiki/Mme "Mme") (Madame)* followed by the name of their offices.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) Thus, the president of the republic is called *M. le président* or *M. le président de la République* if male, and *Mme...* if female. Styles such as "excellency" or similar are not used, except for talking about foreign dignitaries. Traditionally after "*Madame*", the name of the office is not put into the feminine form, but this is becoming less common (hence, "*Madame le président*" is being replaced by "*Madame la présidente*").
* In [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), members of the lower house (Chamber of Deputies) of the [Parliament of Italy](/wiki/Parliament_of_Italy "Parliament of Italy") are styled *Honourable* ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Onorevole*, abbreviation *On.*). The correct form to address a member of the upper house (Senate) is *Senator* ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Senatore*, abbreviation *Sen.*; even though, for gravitas, they may also be addressed *Honourable Senator*).
* The incumbent president of Finland is addressed *Herra/Rouva Tasavallan Presidentti* (Mr./Ms. President of the Republic), while a former president is addressed as just *Herra/Rouva Presidentti*.
* The style used for the [president of Ireland](/wiki/President_of_Ireland "President of Ireland") is normally His Excellency/Her Excellency ([Irish](/wiki/Irish_language "Irish language"): *A Shoilse/A Soilse*); sometimes people may orally address the president as 'Your Excellency' ([Irish](/wiki/Irish_language "Irish language"): *A Shoilse* [ə ˈhəʎʃə]), or simply 'President' ([Irish](/wiki/Irish_language "Irish language"): *A Uachtaráin* [ə ˈuəxt̪ˠəɾˠaːnˠ] (vocative case)).
* During the [Republic of the United Netherlands](/wiki/Republic_of_the_United_Netherlands "Republic of the United Netherlands"), the [States-General](/wiki/States_General_of_the_Netherlands "States General of the Netherlands") were collectively addressed as "Their High and Mighty Lords" ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Hoogmogende Heren*).
* The Honourable – Presidents, prime ministers, ministers, governors, members of parliament, senate and congress in some countries. (Australia, Bangladesh, Canada, India, Hong Kong, Singapore, Sri Lanka.)

### Medicine

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Medicine")]

* Doctor – In the United Kingdom, university degrees required for initial medical and dental professional registration are all [bachelor's degrees](/wiki/Bachelor%27s_degrees "Bachelor's degrees") (commonly [MBBS](/wiki/MBBS "MBBS"), but also MB, BDS, MB BS BAO, BMed, etc.). This system is followed in other countries (often [Commonwealth countries](/wiki/Commonwealth_of_Nations "Commonwealth of Nations")) that adhere to the United Kingdom's higher education tradition. Such graduates are addressed as "doctor" by courtesy and convention.
* Mr, Miss, Ms, or Mrs – Surgeons in the UK, Australia, New Zealand, and other [Commonwealth or former Commonwealth nations](/wiki/Commonwealth_of_Nations "Commonwealth of Nations") revert to the title *Mr*, *Miss*, *Ms* or *Mrs* after obtaining the postgraduate qualification [MRCS](/wiki/Membership_of_the_Royal_College_of_Surgeons "Membership of the Royal College of Surgeons").[[17]](#cite_note-17) Other specialist doctors (["physicians" in the sense](/wiki/Physician#Specialist_in_internal_medicine "Physician") of specialists in different areas of internal medicine, as used generally outside North America), on the other hand, retain the title *Dr* after obtaining other postgraduate qualifications, such as [MRCP](/wiki/Membership_of_the_Royal_College_of_Physicians "Membership of the Royal College of Physicians"). Medical practitioners who are both "physicians and surgeons" are called either *Dr* or *Mr*, *Ms*, etc. and the style used at any specific time may be varied according to the context.[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)

### Nautical and aeronautical

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Nautical and aeronautical")]

* Captain – a person who commands and is responsible for the lives of crew and passengers on a naval or civil vessel or aircraft. In the anglophone navies, *captain* is usually used regardless of the actual rank of the person being addressed. For example, on a naval vessel commanded by someone holding a rank of [lieutenant commander](/wiki/Lieutenant_commander "Lieutenant commander") or lower is addressed as "Captain", in reference to their position in command of the ship, not their military rank. This would apply even to an [enlisted man](/wiki/Enlisted_man "Enlisted man") in charge of a small boat in some navies, whereas in others the title would be [coxswain](/wiki/Coxswain "Coxswain").

* [His Holiness](/wiki/His_Holiness "His Holiness") (abbreviation HH), oral address *Your Holiness*, or *Holy Father* – the [Pope](/wiki/Pope "Pope") and the [Pope Emeritus](/wiki/Pope_Emeritus "Pope Emeritus") (but *Holy Father* is not used for the latter); also the [Syriac Orthodox Patriarch of Antioch and All the East](/wiki/List_of_Syriac_Orthodox_Patriarchs_of_Antioch "List of Syriac Orthodox Patriarchs of Antioch"), [Patriarch of Moscow and All Russia](/wiki/List_of_Metropolitans_and_Patriarchs_of_Moscow "List of Metropolitans and Patriarchs of Moscow"), [Patriarch of Peć and the Serbs](/wiki/List_of_heads_of_the_Serbian_Orthodox_Church "List of heads of the Serbian Orthodox Church"), [Catholicos of All Armenians](/wiki/Catholicos_of_All_Armenians "Catholicos of All Armenians"), [Catholicos-Patriarch of All Georgia](/wiki/Catholicos-Patriarch_of_All_Georgia "Catholicos-Patriarch of All Georgia"), [Catholicos of the Holy See of Cilicia of the Armenian Apostolic Church](/wiki/List_of_Armenian_Catholicoi_of_Cilicia "List of Armenian Catholicoi of Cilicia"), [Malankara Orthodox Catholicos](/wiki/Indian_Orthodox_Church "Indian Orthodox Church") and some other patriarchs of the [Christian Church](/wiki/Christian_Church "Christian Church"), as well as the [Dalai Lama](/wiki/Dalai_Lama "Dalai Lama"), the [Panchen Lama](/wiki/Panchen_Lama "Panchen Lama"), the [Karmapa](/wiki/Karmapa "Karmapa"), the [Sakya Trizin](/wiki/Sakya_Trizin "Sakya Trizin"), and other holders of certain other [Tibetan Buddhist](/wiki/Tibetan_Buddhist "Tibetan Buddhist") lineages.

* His Highness the [Aga Khan](/wiki/Aga_Khan "Aga Khan") (abbreviation *HH the Aga Khan.*), oral address *Your Highness* and then *Sir* – The Imam (spiritual leader) of the Shia Ismaili Muslims.

If they have been elevated to the cardinalate by the Pope, they use the traditional "His Eminence" like other cardinals (more properly and formally, "His Beatitude and Eminence").
* [His Most Eminent Royal Highness](/wiki/His_Eminence "His Eminence") (abbreviation *HMERH*), oral address *Your Most Eminent Royal Highness* - The Lord of the [Rasulid Order](/wiki/Rasulid_dynasty "Rasulid dynasty").
* [His Most Eminent Highness](/wiki/His_Most_Eminent_Highness "His Most Eminent Highness") (abbreviation *HMEH*), oral address *Your Most Eminent Highness* – The [Prince](/wiki/Prince_of_the_Church "Prince of the Church") and [Grand Master](/wiki/Grand_Master_of_the_Knights_Hospitaller "Grand Master of the Knights Hospitaller") of the [Sovereign Military Order of Malta](/wiki/Sovereign_Military_Order_of_Malta "Sovereign Military Order of Malta").
* [His Eminence](/wiki/His_Eminence "His Eminence") (abbreviation "H.Em."), oral address *Your Eminence* or *Most Reverend Eminence* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") [cardinals](/wiki/Cardinal_(Catholicism) "Cardinal (Catholicism)")
* His Eminence (abbreviation "H.Em.") *or* The Most Reverend (abbreviation *The Most Rev.*), oral address *Your Eminence* – [Eastern Orthodox](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox "Eastern Orthodox") [metropolitans](/wiki/Metropolitan_bishop "Metropolitan bishop") and [archbishops](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") who are not the first [hierarch](/wiki/Hierarch "Hierarch") of an [autocephalous](/wiki/Autocephalous "Autocephalous") church;
* His Eminence (abbreviation "H.Em.") – Certain high [lamas](/wiki/Lama "Lama") or [rinpoches](/wiki/Rinpoche "Rinpoche") in [Tibetan Buddhism](/wiki/Tibetan_Buddhism "Tibetan Buddhism") as well as presiding head bishops or priests of [Japanese Buddhist](/wiki/Japanese_Buddhism "Japanese Buddhism") schools.
* His Eminence (abbreviation "H.Em.") – The [Sultan of Sokoto](/wiki/Sultan_of_Sokoto "Sultan of Sokoto"), spiritual leader of [Nigeria](/wiki/Nigeria "Nigeria")'s Muslims, as well as those of his fellow [Fula](/wiki/Fula_people "Fula people") high chiefs that choose not to style themselves as HRHs.

Hon.*), oral address *Your Grace* – [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England") (Anglican) [archbishops](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") who are [privy counsellors](/wiki/Privy_council "Privy council"), usually the [Archbishops](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop") of [Canterbury](/wiki/Archbishop_of_Canterbury "Archbishop of Canterbury") and [York](/wiki/Archbishop_of_York "Archbishop of York")
* The Most Reverend (abbreviation *The Most Rev.*), oral address *Your Grace* – [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican_Communion "Anglican Communion") [archbishops](/wiki/Archbishop "Archbishop"), [primates](/wiki/Primate_(bishop)#Anglican_Communion "Primate (bishop)"), [metropolitans](/wiki/Metropolitan_bishop#Anglican "Metropolitan bishop") and [presiding bishops](/wiki/Presiding_Bishop#Anglican "Presiding Bishop"). Canadian Anglican (arch)bishops are orally addressed simply as *Archbishop/Bishop*.[[20]](#cite_note-canada-20) Also [moderators](/wiki/Moderators_and_clerks_in_the_Church_of_Scotland#Moderators "Moderators and clerks in the Church of Scotland").
* The Most Reverend (abbreviation *The Most Rev.*), oral address *My Lord* – [Church of Ireland](/wiki/Church_of_Ireland "Church of Ireland") (Anglican) [Bishop of Meath and Kildare](/wiki/Bishop_of_Meath_and_Kildare "Bishop of Meath and Kildare") (due to being, historically, the most senior bishop in Ireland)

* The Most Reverend (abbreviation *The Most Rev.*), oral address *Bishop* – the Presiding Bishop of the [Episcopal Church in the United States](/wiki/Episcopal_Church_(United_States) "Episcopal Church (United States)")[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* The Right Reverend and Right Honourable [Monsignor](/wiki/Monsignor "Monsignor") (abbreviation *The Rt. Rev. and Rt.mHon. Mgr.*), oral address *Monsignor*, or according to personal preference – Prelate of Honour who is also a [privy counsellor](/wiki/Privy_Council "Privy Council") (The Right Reverend and Right Honourable Monsignor [Graham Leonard](/wiki/Graham_Leonard "Graham Leonard") KCVO).
* The Right Reverend and Right Honourable (abbreviation *The Rt. Rev. and Rt. Hon.*), oral address *Bishop* or *My Lord* (old-fashioned) – [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England") (Anglican) [bishops](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") who are members of the [Privy Council](/wiki/Privy_Council "Privy Council"), usually the [Bishop of London](/wiki/Bishop_of_London "Bishop of London").
* The Right Reverend (abbreviation *The Rt. Rev.*), oral address *Bishop* or *My Lord* (old-fashioned) – other [Church of England](/wiki/Church_of_England "Church of England") [bishops](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop")
* The Right Reverend (abbreviation *The Rt. Rev.*), oral address *Bishop* – [bishops](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") [Episcopal Church (United States)](/wiki/Episcopal_Church_(United_States) "Episcopal Church (United States)")[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* The Right Reverend (abbreviation *The Rt. Rev.*), oral address *Mr./Mrs./Ms./Miss (surname)*[[20]](#cite_note-canada-20) – [Moderator of the United Church of Canada](/wiki/Moderator_of_the_United_Church_of_Canada "Moderator of the United Church of Canada") or of the [Presbyterian Church in Canada](/wiki/Presbyterian_Church_in_Canada "Presbyterian Church in Canada")
* The Right Reverend Father (abbreviation *The Rt. Rev. Fr.*), oral address *Father* – [Eastern Orthodox](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox_Church "Eastern Orthodox Church") [archimandrites](/wiki/Archimandrite "Archimandrite").
* The Right Reverend (abbreviation *The Rt. Rev.*), oral address *Father* or *Father Abbot* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") [abbots](/wiki/Abbot "Abbot").
* The Right Reverend (abbreviation *The Rt Rev.*), oral address *Bishop* – diocesan [bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") of the [Methodist Church Ghana](/wiki/Methodist_Church_Ghana "Methodist Church Ghana")
* Bishop, oral address *Bishop* – an area [bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop") in the [United Methodist Church](/wiki/United_Methodist_Church "United Methodist Church"). The Right Reverend has never been pervasive in the United Methodist Church.
* [The Very Reverend](/wiki/The_Very_Reverend "The Very Reverend") (abbreviation *The Very Rev.* ), oral address *Father* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") [vicars general](/wiki/Vicars_general "Vicars general"), judicial vicars, [judges](/wiki/Judge "Judge"), rectors of seminaries, [vicars forane](/wiki/Vicar_forane "Vicar forane"), episcopal vicars, general superiors of religious orders of priests, provincial superiors, priors of monasteries or friaries
* The Very Reverend Father (abbreviation *The Very Rev. Fr.*), oral address *Father* – [Eastern Orthodox](/wiki/Eastern_Orthodox_Church "Eastern Orthodox Church") [archpriests](/wiki/Archpriest "Archpriest")
* The Very Reverend (abbreviation *The Very Rev.*), oral address *Mr./Madam Dean* or *Mr./Madam Provost*, as appropriate, or *Very Reverend Sir/Madam* – [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") [deans](/wiki/Dean_(Christianity) "Dean (Christianity)") and [provosts](/wiki/Provost_(religion)#Anglican_Church "Provost (religion)") of [cathedrals](/wiki/Cathedral "Cathedral"), the deans of [Westminster Abbey](/wiki/Westminster_Abbey "Westminster Abbey") and [St George's Chapel, Windsor](/wiki/St._George%27s_Chapel,_Windsor_Castle "St. George's Chapel, Windsor Castle"), and, for historical reasons, a few [parish priests](/wiki/Incumbent_(ecclesiastical) "Incumbent (ecclesiastical)"), such as the [Dean of Bocking](/wiki/Braintree,_Essex "Braintree, Essex"). Sometimes an Anglican cathedral dean has previously been a [bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop"), in which case he or she is styled as a bishop, but on formal occasions may be addressed *Mr./Madam Dean*. Canadian deans are orally addressed as *Dean* only.[[20]](#cite_note-canada-20)
* The Very Reverend (abbreviation *The Very Rev.*), oral address *Very Reverend Sir/Madam* or *Mr./Madam Dean* – [Deans](/wiki/Dean_(Christianity) "Dean (Christianity)") of some Anglican [seminaries](/wiki/Seminaries "Seminaries"), especially [those in the United States](/wiki/Episcopal_Church_(United_States) "Episcopal Church (United States)")
* The Very Reverend (abbreviation *The Very Rev.*), oral address *Osofo Panin* – Superintendent minister in the [Methodist Church Ghana](/wiki/Methodist_Church_Ghana "Methodist Church Ghana")
* The Very Reverend (abbreviation *The Very Rev.*), oral address *Reverend* – former [moderators](/wiki/Moderator_of_the_United_Church_of_Canada "Moderator of the United Church of Canada") of the [United Church of Canada](/wiki/United_Church_of_Canada "United Church of Canada") and of the Presbyterian Church in Canada; the Canadian government prescribes the oral address *Mr./Mrs./Ms. (surname)*[[20]](#cite_note-canada-20)
* The Reverend Monsignor (abbreviation *The Rev. Msgr.*), oral address *Monsignor* – [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") protonotaries apostolic, honorary prelates, chaplains of his holiness
* [The Venerable](/wiki/The_Venerable "The Venerable"), oral address *Venerable Sir/Madam* or *Mr./Madam Archdeacon* – [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") archdeacons; in Canada, they are orally addressed as *Archdeacon* only[[20]](#cite_note-canada-20)
* Venerable (abbreviation "Ven."), oral address "Venerable" or "Venerable <name or title>" – fully ordained [Buddhist monks](/wiki/Bhikkhu "Bhikkhu") and [nuns](/wiki/Bhikkhuni "Bhikkhuni"), the title of *Venerable Master* or *Most Venerable* is sometimes appended for senior monks and nuns or monks/nuns acting in their capacity as an abbot/abbess of a monastery
* The Reverend and Right Honourable (abbreviation *The Revd. and Rt. Hon.*) – [Protestant](/wiki/Protestant "Protestant") ordained ministers who are members of the [Privy Council](/wiki/Privy_Council "Privy Council") (e.g. the late [Ian Paisley](/wiki/Ian_Paisley "Ian Paisley"))
* The Reverend the Honourable (abbreviation *The Rev. the Hon.*), oral address according to ecclesiastical or other status – ordained son of an earl, viscount, or baron, or ordained daughter of a viscount or baron (unless also a privy counsellor or peer)
* The Very Reverend (abbreviation "The Very Rev."), oral address: "Overseer" – in the Anglican-Apostolic Communion (Pentecostal) tradition, the overseer is the lowest level of prelate (only non–consecrated bishop prelate), with oversight to a specific work or department, directly responsible to the primate/presiding bishop or an ordinary/diocesan bishop.
* The Reverend (abbreviation *The Rev.* or *The Rev'd*) – Protestant and Anglican ordained ministers (common variants include *Pastor*, *Parson*, *Vicar*, or simply *Reverend* (Rev.), as used in American English; see: [The Reverend](/wiki/The_Reverend "The Reverend")); some Jewish cantors also use this style, almost all Buddhist ministers in Japan use this style
* The Reverend Canon (abbreviation *The Rev. Canon*), oral address *Canon* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") and [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") canons
* The Reverend Doctor (abbreviation *The Rev. Dr.*), oral address *Father* or *Doctor* – [Priests](/wiki/Priest "Priest") and other ordained clergy with a [doctorate](/wiki/Doctorate "Doctorate")
* The Reverend Father (abbreviation *The Rev. Fr.*), oral address *Father* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") (and many [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican")) [priests](/wiki/Priest "Priest")
* The Reverend Mother (abbreviation *The Rev. Mo.*), oral address *Mother* – [Abbesses](/wiki/Abbess "Abbess") (also, some female [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") [priests](/wiki/Priest "Priest") {abbreviated Mthr.})
* The Reverend Deacon (abbreviation *Rev. Deacon*), oral address *Deacon* [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") permanent Deacons.
* The Reverend Mister (abbreviation *The Rev. Mr.*), oral address *Deacon* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") transitional deacons, i.e. those preparing for priesthood. Transitional deacons belonging to religious orders (monastic and non-monastic) are titled Reverend Brother, (similar situations and modifications apply to [Anglican](/wiki/Anglican "Anglican") deacons as in *The Rev. Fr./Mthr.,* above; since women can be deacons, these may be *The Revd Ms.*)
* Mother, oral address *Mother* – heads of some female [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") religious convents and other communities who are not abbesses
* Mister (abbreviation *Mr.*), oral address *Mister* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") [Sulpician](/wiki/Sulpician "Sulpician") priests
* Mister (abbreviation *Mr.*), oral address often *Mister* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") seminarians and scholastics (members preparing for priesthood) of *some* religious orders (notably, [Jesuits](/wiki/Jesuits "Jesuits")).
* Father (pater)
* Brother (abbreviation *Bro.*), oral address *Brother* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") members of religious orders under vows (both monastic and non-monastic) who are not priests.
* Sister (abbreviation *Sr.*), oral address *Sister* – [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") members of religious orders under vows (both monastic and non-monastic) who are not abbesses.
* [Elder](/wiki/Elder_(Latter_Day_Saints) "Elder (Latter Day Saints)"): used generally for male missionaries of [the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints](/wiki/The_Church_of_Jesus_Christ_of_Latter-day_Saints "The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints") (LDS Church) and for members of the adult leadership known as the general authorities. Although most all male adults of the LDS church are elders, the title is reserved for the prior mentioned groups.[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* Grand Rabbi, oral address *Rabbi* – [Hasidic](/wiki/Hasidic_Judaism "Hasidic Judaism") [rabbis](/wiki/Rabbi "Rabbi"), who are scions of a Hasidic Dynasty.
* [Dom](/wiki/Dom_(title) "Dom (title)") is an [honorific](/wiki/Honorific "Honorific") [prefixed](/wiki/Prefix "Prefix") to the [given name](/wiki/Given_name "Given name"). It derives from the Latin *[dominus](/wiki/Dominus_(title) "Dominus (title)")*. It is used in English for certain [Benedictine](/wiki/Benedictine "Benedictine") (including some communities which follow the [Rule of St. Benedict](/wiki/Rule_of_St._Benedict "Rule of St. Benedict")) and [Carthusian](/wiki/Carthusian "Carthusian") [monks](/wiki/Monks "Monks"), and for members of certain communities of [Canons Regular](/wiki/Canons_Regular "Canons Regular"). Examples include Benedictine monks of the [English Benedictine Congregation](/wiki/English_Benedictine_Congregation "English Benedictine Congregation") (e.g. [Dom John Chapman](/wiki/John_Chapman_OSB "John Chapman OSB"), late [Abbot of Downside](/wiki/Downside_Abbey "Downside Abbey")). The equivalent female usage for such a monastic is "[Dame](/wiki/Dame_(title) "Dame (title)")" (e.g. Dame [Laurentia McLachlan](/wiki/Laurentia_McLachlan "Laurentia McLachlan"), late [Abbess of Stanbrook](/wiki/Stanbrook_Abbey "Stanbrook Abbey"), or Dame [Felicitas Corrigan](/wiki/Felicitas_Corrigan "Felicitas Corrigan"), author).
  + *Dom* has historically been used on occasions in French, as an honorific for Benedictine monks, such as the famous *[Dom Pérignon](/wiki/Dom_P%C3%A9rignon_(monk) "Dom Pérignon (monk)")*.
* Rabbi, oral address *Rabbi* (or, if holder of the appropriate degree, *Doctor* both in oral and written communication) – rabbis
* Grand Ayatullah, oral address *Ayatullah* or *Ayatullah al-Uzma* – [Shia](/wiki/Shiite_Islam "Shiite Islam") [Ayatullahs](/wiki/Ayatullah "Ayatullah"), who have accomplished the highest religious jurisprudent knowledge degree called as [marja'](/wiki/Marja%27 "Marja'") and some people officially follow them.
* Ayatullah, oral address *Ayatullah* – [Shia](/wiki/Shiite_Islam "Shiite Islam") religious degree who has accomplished a religious high course of lessons and is capable of individually issuing religious verdicts.
* [Seghatoleslam](/wiki/Seghatoleslam "Seghatoleslam"), is an honorific title within the [Twelver](/wiki/Twelver_Shi%27ism "Twelver Shi'ism") [Shia clergy](/wiki/Shia_clergy "Shia clergy"). Seghatoleslam designates [narrators](/wiki/The_Narrators "The Narrators") whose justice and [trustworthiness](/wiki/Trustworthiness "Trustworthiness") have been explicitly verified.
* Amīr al-Mu'minīn ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): أمير المؤمنين), usually translated Commander of the Faithful or Leader of the Faithful, is the Arabic style of some [Caliphs](/wiki/Caliphate "Caliphate") and other independent sovereign [Muslim](/wiki/Muslim "Muslim") rulers that claim legitimacy from a community of Muslims. It has been claimed as the title of rulers in Muslim countries and empires and is still used for some Muslim leaders. The use of the title does not necessarily signify a claim to caliphate as it is usually taken to be, but described a certain form of activist leadership which may have been attached to a caliph but also could signify a level of authority beneath that. The Ottoman sultans, in particular, made scant use of it. Moreover, the term was used by men who made no claim to be caliphs.[[13]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-13) Currently used by the Caliph of [ISIS](/wiki/ISIS "ISIS") [Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi](/wiki/Abu_Bakr_al-Baghdadi "Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi"), The Ahmadiyya Muslim Caliph, [Mirza Masroor Ahmad](/wiki/Mirza_Masroor_Ahmad "Mirza Masroor Ahmad"),[[14]](#cite_note-Valentine_208-14) the [King of Morocco](/wiki/King_of_Morocco "King of Morocco"), The [Sultan of Sokoto](/wiki/Sultan_of_Sokoto "Sultan of Sokoto"), The supreme leaders of the Afghan [Taliban](/wiki/Taliban "Taliban").
* Cantor, oral address *Cantor* (some cantors use *The Reverend* as style, as above) – Jewish [cantors](/wiki/Hazzan "Hazzan")
* Reverend, oral address *Reverend*, *Mister* or *Brother* – ordained ministers/pastors
* Pastor (abbreviation "Pr."), oral address 'Pastor" – minister responsible for caring for the "flock" in Lutheran churches[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* Pandit (sometimes spelled *Pundit*) – [Hindu](/wiki/Hinduism "Hinduism") priests
* [Swami](/wiki/Swami "Swami") – in [Hinduism](/wiki/Hinduism "Hinduism") an ascetic or yogi who has been initiated into a religious monastic order. Informally, "Swamiji".
* Officers of [The Salvation Army](/wiki/The_Salvation_Army "The Salvation Army") are addressed by their rank, e.g. "Captain" (Capt.), "Major" (Maj.), etc.
* A wide variety of titles for Neo-pagan religions; Lord/Lady, Father/Mother, and High Priest/Priestess are common

### Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Australia")]

* His/Her Majesty – The [King or Queen of Australia](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Australia "Monarchy of Australia")
* His/Her Excellency [The Honourable](/wiki/The_Honourable "The Honourable") – [Governor-General](/wiki/Governor-General_of_Australia "Governor-General of Australia") and his or her spouse,[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26) and The Honourable or His/Her Excellency for the rest of state governors (but not their spouse)
* The Honourable – all current and former [governors-general](/wiki/Governor-General_of_Australia "Governor-General of Australia") and Administrators of the Northern Territory, Justices of the [High Court of Australia](/wiki/High_Court_of_Australia "High Court of Australia"), the [Federal Court of Australia](/wiki/Federal_Court_of_Australia "Federal Court of Australia"), the [Family Court of Australia](/wiki/Family_Court_of_Australia "Family Court of Australia") and state supreme courts
* The Honourable – all current and former members of the Federal Executive Council and all current members of state [executive councils](/wiki/Executive_Council_(Commonwealth_countries) "Executive Council (Commonwealth countries)") and certain former members of state executive councils and long-serving members of state Legislative Councils (upper houses of state parliaments) that have been given the right to keep the title by permission of the governor of that state.

* The Right Honourable the Lord Mayor – Lord mayors of Australian cities
* His/Her Worship – Administrators of territories (obsolete), magistrates (obsolete) and mayors.

### Brunei

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Brunei")]

Known as *terasul* in the Malay language.

* *Kebawah Duli Yang Maha Mulia (Kebawah DYMM)*, equivalent to His or [Her Majesty](/wiki/Majesty "Majesty") (HM) – for [Sultan](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei") and his first royal consort. The style is added more depends on the situation:
  + Kebawah Duli Yang Maha Mulia Paduka Seri Sultan, for Sultan before coronation.
  + Kebawah Duli Yang Maha Mulia Paduka Seri Baginda Sultan, for Sultan after coronation.
  + Kebawah Duli Yang Maha Mulia Paduka Seri Pengiran Isteri for the queen consort before coronation
  + Kebawah Duli Yang Maha Mulia Paduka Seri Baginda Raja Isteri for the queen consort after coronation
  + Kebawah Duli Yang Maha Mulia Paduka Seri Raja — for the second wife of the Sultan during coronation
* *Kebawah Duli*, for a Sultan that has not gone through puberty.
* *Duli Yang Maha Mulia Paduka Seri Pengiran Isteri*, for the second wife of the Sultan after coronation
* *Duli Yang Teramat Mulia (DYTM)*, equivalent to His or [Her Royal Highness](/wiki/Royal_Highness "Royal Highness") (HRH) – for the Crown Prince and his consort and for the abdicated Sultan and his consort.
  + Duli Yang Teramat Mulia Paduka Seri Begawan Sultan — for Sultan that abdicated from the throne
  + Duli Yang Teramat Mulia Paduka Suri Seri Begawan Raja — for the Sultan's consort when the Sultan abdicated from the throne
  + Duli Yang Teramat Mulia Paduka Seri Pengiran Muda Mahkota — for the Crown Prince
  + Duli Yang Teramat Mulia Paduka Seri Pengiran Anak Isteri — for the Crown Prince's consort
* *Yang Teramat Mulia (YTM)*, to His or [Her Royal Highness](/wiki/Royal_Highness "Royal Highness") (HRH) – for the children of the Sultan that were born by their royal mother (both parents of the royal mother are royalties and not a commoner)
  + Yang Teramat Mulia Paduka Seri Duli Pengiran Muda — for the Sultan's son that has full royal blood
  + Yang Teramat Mulia Paduka Seri Pengiran Anak Puteri— for the Sultan's daughter by a royal mother (non-commoner)
  + Yang Teramat Mulia Pengiran Babu Raja — for the Queen Consort's mother
* *Yang Amat Mulia (YAM)*, for the consort of a royal prince and their children, and for the Sultan's children by their commoner mother
  + Yang Amat Mulia Pengiran Anak Isteri — for the consort of the Sultan's son (full royal blood)
  + Yang Amat Mulia Pengiran Muda — for the son (full royal blood) of the Sultan's son (full royal blood)
  + Yang Amat Mulia Pengiran Anak — for the children of the Sultan that were born by a commoner mother; daughter (full royal blood) of the Sultan's son (full royal blood); children (full royal blood) of the Sultan's daughter (full royal blood); children (full royal blood) of the Sultan's children (half royal blood)
* *Yang Mulia (YM)*
  + Yang Mulia Pengiran Anak — for the children that both parents hold the title *Pengiran Anak*
  + Yang Mulia Pengiran — for the children of a Pengiran Anak and his wife that is not also a Pengiran Anak; non-royal Pengiran (a commoner Pengiran)

See also: [Canadian honorifics](/wiki/Canadian_honorifics "Canadian honorifics")

* His/Her Majesty – [King/Queen of Canada](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Canada "Monarchy of Canada")
* His/Her Excellency – [Governor General](/wiki/Governor_General_of_Canada "Governor General of Canada"), [vice-regal consort](/wiki/Viceregal_consort_of_Canada "Viceregal consort of Canada"), [ambassadors](/wiki/Ambassador "Ambassador"), and [high commissioners](/wiki/High_Commissioner_(Commonwealth) "High Commissioner (Commonwealth)") *in office*
* [The Right Honourable](/wiki/The_Right_Honourable "The Right Honourable") – Governors general, [prime ministers](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Canada "Prime Minister of Canada"), [chief justices of Canada](/wiki/Chief_Justice_of_Canada "Chief Justice of Canada") and certain eminent Canadians *for life*
* His/Her Honour – [Lieutenant-governors](/wiki/Lieutenant_governor_(Canada) "Lieutenant governor (Canada)") and viceregal consorts *in office*
* The Honourable
  + *For life* – Members of the [King's Privy Council for Canada](/wiki/King%27s_Privy_Council_for_Canada "King's Privy Council for Canada"), [senators](/wiki/Senate_of_Canada "Senate of Canada"), lieutenant-governors, and members and honorary members of the Executive Councils [of Nova Scotia](/wiki/Executive_Council_of_Nova_Scotia "Executive Council of Nova Scotia") and [of Alberta](/wiki/Executive_Council_of_Alberta "Executive Council of Alberta")
  + *In office only* – [Speaker of the House of Commons](/wiki/Speaker_of_the_House_of_Commons_of_Canada "Speaker of the House of Commons of Canada"), [ministers of the Crown](/wiki/Minister_of_the_Crown "Minister of the Crown") (however federal ministers invariably enter the Privy Council upon their initial appointment, thus assuming the honorific for life), judges of provincial courts, [territorial commissioners](/wiki/Commissioner#Canadian_territories "Commissioner"), and members of other provincial and territorial executive councils
    - Note: Members of Parliament are often referred to in the House of Commons as "the honourable member for ([riding](/wiki/Electoral_district_(Canada) "Electoral district (Canada)"))" but do not use the style *honourable* with their name.
* The Honourable Mr./Madam Justice – Chief justices of province and justices of superior courts
* His/Her Worship – Justices of the peace, magistrates and municipal leaders *in office*

### New Zealand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: New Zealand")]

### India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: India")]

*His Excellency/Her Excellency* is used before the name of President of India as well as before of governors of the states. However, it is not mandatory for an Indian citizen to use this style to address the president or the governors after a notification from the President House. But it is mandatory for foreigners to address the president and governors.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

*Your Honour/My Lord* – It is used before the names of judges but now it is also not mandatory. The [Supreme Court](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_India "Supreme Court of India") in a hearing said that people need to respect the judges and "Sir" is sufficient for it.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

#### Royal styles in India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Royal styles in India")]

With a long history of rulers, there are many styles which vary from territory to territory and languages for royal families in India, commonly [Maharaja](/wiki/Maharaja "Maharaja") (for king), [Maharani](/wiki/Maharani "Maharani") (queen) whereas for their successors Raja, Rani (Maha meaning "Great" removed). Rajkumar (for prince) and Rajkumari (for princess).

Others include [Hukam](/wiki/Hukam "Hukam") (commonly in [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan")), [Sardar](/wiki/Sardar "Sardar") (kings in territories of Punjab within [Sikh Empire](/wiki/Sikh_Empire "Sikh Empire")), [Badshah](/wiki/Padishah "Padishah") ([Mughal Empire](/wiki/Mughal_Empire "Mughal Empire")), Vazeer-e-Aala (in [Mughal Empire](/wiki/Mughal_Empire "Mughal Empire")) etc.

### African traditional rulers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: African traditional rulers")]

In most of [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa"), many styles are used by [traditional royalty](/wiki/Monarchies_in_Africa "Monarchies in Africa").

Generally the vast majority of the members of these royal families use the titles Prince and Princess, while the higher ranked amongst them also use either [Highness](/wiki/Highness "Highness") or [Royal Highness](/wiki/Royal_Highness "Royal Highness") to describe secondary appellations in their native languages that they hold in their realms, appellations that are intended to highlight their relative proximity to their thrones, either literally in the sense of the extant [kingships](/wiki/Kingship "Kingship") of the continent or symbolically in the sense of its varied [chiefships of the name](/wiki/Chief_of_the_Name "Chief of the Name"), and which therefore serve a function similar to the said styles of Highness and Royal Highness.

For example, the [Yoruba people](/wiki/Yoruba_people "Yoruba people") of [West Africa](/wiki/West_Africa "West Africa") usually make use of the word *Kabiyesi* when speaking either to or about their [sovereigns](/wiki/Monarch "Monarch") and other royals. As such, it is variously translated as Majesty, Royal Highness or Highness depending on the actual rank of the person in question, though a literal translation of the word would read more like this: "He (or She) whose words are beyond questioning, Great Lawgiver of the Nation."

Within the [Zulu Kingdom](/wiki/Zulu_Kingdom "Zulu Kingdom") of [Southern Africa](/wiki/Southern_Africa "Southern Africa"), meanwhile, the monarch and other senior royals are often addressed as *uNdabezitha* meaning "He (or She) Who Concerns the Enemy", but rendered in English as Majesty in address or reference to the king and his consorts, or Royal Highness in the case of other senior members of the royal family.

### Hong Kong

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Hong Kong")]

The [Chief Executive](/wiki/Chief_Executive_of_Hong_Kong "Chief Executive of Hong Kong") is styled as The [Honourable](/wiki/Honourable "Honourable").

Certain senior government officials (such as the [Chief Secretary for Administration](/wiki/Chief_Secretary_for_Administration "Chief Secretary for Administration")), [President of the Legislative Council](/wiki/President_of_the_Legislative_Council_of_Hong_Kong "President of the Legislative Council of Hong Kong"), members of the [Executive Council](/wiki/Executive_Council_of_Hong_Kong "Executive Council of Hong Kong"), and members of the judiciary (such as the [Chief Justice of the Court of Final Appeal](/wiki/Chief_Justice_of_the_Court_of_Final_Appeal "Chief Justice of the Court of Final Appeal")) are also styled as *The Honourable*.

### Ireland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Ireland")]

In [Ireland](/wiki/Republic_of_Ireland "Republic of Ireland"), holders of offices with [Irish](/wiki/Irish_language "Irish language") names are usually addressed in English by its [nominative form](/wiki/Irish_declension#Nominative "Irish declension") (so, '[Taoiseach](/wiki/Taoiseach "Taoiseach")' and '[Tánaiste](/wiki/T%C3%A1naiste "Tánaiste")'), though the [Irish vocative forms](/wiki/Irish_declension#Vocative "Irish declension") differ (*a Thaoisigh* and *a Thánaiste*). [The President may be styled](/wiki/President_of_Ireland#Official_residence,_salute,_style_and_address "President of Ireland") 'His/Her Excellency' ([Irish](/wiki/Irish_language "Irish language"): *A Shoilse*, IPA: [[ə ˈhəil̠ʲʃə]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Irish "Help:IPA/Irish") / *A Soilse* [[ə ˈsˠəl̠ʲʃə]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Irish "Help:IPA/Irish")) and addressed 'Your Excellency' (Irish: *A Shoilse*), or simply 'President' (Irish: *A Uachtaráin* [[ə ˈuəxt̪ˠəɾˠaːnʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Irish "Help:IPA/Irish")). The titles '[Minister](/wiki/Government_of_Ireland "Government of Ireland")' and '[Senator](/wiki/Seanad_%C3%89ireann "Seanad Éireann")' are used as forms of address; only the latter as a style. A [TD (*Teachta Dála*)](/wiki/Teachta_D%C3%A1la "Teachta Dála") is formally addressed and styled as 'Deputy', though often simply *Mr.*, *Mrs.*, etc. Similarly, [county and city councillors](/wiki/Local_government_in_the_Republic_of_Ireland "Local government in the Republic of Ireland") can be addressed as 'Councillor', abbreviated *Cllr.* which is used as a written style, but are just as frequently addressed as *Mr.*, *Mrs.,* etc.

* *Yang Dihormati (YDh.)*, equivalent to [the Honourable](/wiki/The_Honourable "The Honourable"), is for*—*
  + selected Orang Besar Negeri of Perak

* *Yang Amat Berbahagia (YABhg.)* is for*—*

  + the Orang Besar Empat of Perak
  + spouses of *Yang Amat Dihormati*

* *Yang Berbahagia (YBhg.)* is for*—*
  + the top officials of the [Federal Government](/wiki/Government_of_Malaysia "Government of Malaysia"), namely [Chief Secretary to the Government](/wiki/Chief_Secretary_to_the_Government_of_Malaysia "Chief Secretary to the Government of Malaysia"), [Attorney General](/wiki/Attorney_General_of_Malaysia "Attorney General of Malaysia"), [Chief of Defence Force](/wiki/Chief_of_Defence_Forces_(Malaysia) "Chief of Defence Forces (Malaysia)"), [Inspector-General of Police](/wiki/Inspector-General_of_Police_(Malaysia) "Inspector-General of Police (Malaysia)"), Director General of Public Service and Secretary General of Ministries, with or without any title

* *Yang Amat Berusaha (YABrs.)* is for higher public officers without any title in [Kelantan](/wiki/Kelantan "Kelantan").

### Morocco

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Style_(form_of_address)&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Morocco")]

* His Majesty – The [King of Morocco](/wiki/King_of_Morocco "King of Morocco").
* His Imperial Majesty – The [Sultan of Morocco](/wiki/King_of_Morocco "King of Morocco") (before 1957, now obsolete).
